text: The crypto market is showing signs of recovery with Bitcoin nearing $80,000 and positive inflows into BTC ETFs. Despite ongoing DeFi exploits and regulatory scrutiny, institutional and retail interest in crypto remains strong. Notably, Hyperliquid continues to hold attention with whale accumulation and volume, while prediction markets and AI-driven trading bots are expanding. The market narrative is shifting towards real-world utility and sustainable growth rather than pure hype.
author: elfa_2048047308621648137

text: The Trump Organization's $TRUMP memecoin is being used as a tool for political access and monetization, with top token holders gaining VIP access to private events including a crypto conference at Mar a Lago featuring Donald Trump. The token's value and hype are declining, but the extraction model persists, raising concerns about the intersection of speculative crypto assets and political influence.
author: elfa_2048021632342528115

text: A Solana memecoin project called $CULT exploited X's (formerly Twitter) gold badge system by distributing affiliate badges to hundreds of supporters, creating a rapid hype spike and a $400k market cap in 48 hours. The main account was suspended for inauthentic coordination, causing the token to crash and leaving badge holders at risk of account bans due to inability to remove badges. This incident highlights risks of social media growth hacks and short-lived hype in crypto communities.
author: elfa_2048170995672809653

text: Purrlend protocol suffered a significant exploit on both MegaETH and HyperEVM chains, resulting in a combined loss of approximately $1.5 million in various stablecoins and tokens including USDC, USDT0, USDH, and wrapped HYPE tokens. Users are advised to avoid interacting with Purrlend until further information is provided.
author: elfa_2048049351881097541

text: Printr, an omnichain token launchpad, is gaining attention for its existing usage metrics including 15,000+ tokens launched, 150M+ trading volume, and 50,000+ daily active users. Printr V2 introduces features like Proof of Belief staking, custom fee routing, and cross-chain swaps, aiming to improve creator alignment and token utility beyond initial hype. The public sale of $PRINT token at a $50M FDV is underway, with cautious optimism about its potential to sustain volume and utility post-TGE amid a competitive launchpad market.
author: elfa_2047929589234827592

text: Hyperliquid's HIP-3 upgrade has driven a surge in onchain commodity perpetual markets, reaching record open interest of $2.38B and dominating the onchain commodity perps space. The platform supports a wide range of commodities including gold, silver, and oil, enabling 24/7 trading and real-world asset exposure. This growth is part of a broader trend with competitors like Polymarket and Kalshi entering the commodity perps market, though Hyperliquid remains the dominant player with superior infrastructure.
author: elfa_2047982384319246645

text: Hyperliquid (token $HYPE) continues to show strong market performance and user interest, with notable whale accumulation, staking rewards, and a growing NFT ecosystem including Hypurrs. The platform is recognized for its efficient trading infrastructure, high profit per employee, and innovative features like HIP-3 enabling permissionless perpetual markets, especially in commodities and real-world assets. Despite competition from new entrants like Polymarket launching perpetual futures, Hyperliquid maintains a moat with its real orderbook, sub-second execution, and integrated prediction markets. Upcoming token unlocks for $HYPE are significant but currently low-impact on supply.
author: elfa_2048166692946317470

text: Highlight Clip CZ: With My Chinese Background, My Parents Always Emphasized Saving Binance founder CZ shared that his Chinese upbringing instilled in him the importance of saving, no matter how little you earn. He advises people to cut out meaningless expenses and carve out time every day to learn new skills. Drawing from his own days working as a dishwasher, CZ emphasized that by resisting the short-term dopamine of overspending, staying optimistic, and committing to long-term growth, anyone can ultimately regain control of their life and achieve financial freedom. — link
author: admin#1534592031

text: Litecoin Reorg Reverses Invalid MWEB Transactions After Zero-Day Exploit Litecoin said a zero-day vulnerability in its MimbleWimble Extension Block (MWEB) allowed non-updated nodes to validate an invalid transaction, enabling attackers to peg out funds and trigger a DoS attack on mining pools. The network executed a 13-block reorganization to reverse the invalid transactions, while valid transactions remained unaffected. The vulnerability has since been fully patched and the network is operating normally. — link

text: The Shape of a Market: The Case for Kraken At $20B, Kraken looks fairly priced as a crypto exchange but the market is underweighting the regulatory and infrastructure stack it has quietly assembled - Fed Master Account, all three CFTC licenses via Bitnomial, the largest tokenized equities product globally (xStocks), the Backed Finance acquisition, a Nasdaq tokenization partnership, and its own L2 (Ink). The author's bet is asymmetric: downside is anchored by the exchange floor, while upside is open-ended if any of three independent catalysts - Bitnomial clearing scale, US xStocks approval, or banking products on the Fed account - execute.

text: The Stanford Freshmen Who Want to Rule the World Stanford has become an "incubator with dorms," where VCs aggressively court 19 year olds with pre-idea funding, secret clubs, and lavish dinners This turns a select cohort of undergrads into the targets of a high-stakes talent rush, while the broader student body increasingly mimics the dropout-founder mythology. The piece however argues this system breeds shallow ambition, lax oversight, and recurring fraud (Theranos, Clinkle, Do Kwon), as money chases hype rather than substance
author: admin#1742094092

text: Anthropic - Project Deal Anthropic ran "Project Deal," a week's experiment where employees got a $100 budget and had Claude agents operated in a marketplce to conduct interviews to learn preferences, then autonomously listed items, negotiated, and closed deals (186 of them, totaling ~$4,000) without human intervention The twist: some participants were secretly assigned either Claude Opus 4.5 or the weaker Haiku 4.5. Results showed that Opus agents objectively outperformed - closing more deals, selling the same items for ~$3.64 more on average, and paying less as buyers. But participants represented by Haiku didn't notice they were getting worse outcomes, rating fairness and satisfaction roughly the same This represents a near-future agent-to-agent economy, with both upside (lower friction, real value to users) and concerns: quality gaps between agents could quietly entrench inequality, and corporate incentives, prompt injection, and jailbreaking could distort outcomes in ways the legal and policy frameworks aren't ready for

text: France Sees Surge in Crypto-Related Kidnappings, 88 Charged According to Le Monde, France's national anti-organized crime prosecutor said crypto-related kidnapping cases are rapidly increasing. A total of 88 individuals have been charged across 12 cases, including over 10 minors, with 75 held in pretrial detention. Authorities have recorded 135 such incidents since 2023, including 18 in 2024, 67 in 2025, and 47 so far in 2026. These cases typically involve abduction or unlawful detention, with victims forced to transfer crypto assets as ransom. — link

text: Highlight Clip Bybit CEO Ben: DeFi's in Its Regulatory Honeymoon Period During the fireside chat between Brian McGleenon, Global News Head of BeInCrypto, and Ben Zhou, Co-founder & CEO of Bybit, at Paris Blockchain Week 2026, Ben Zhou pointed out that the cross-border and decentralized nature of DeFi is inherently contradictory to centralized regulation. At present, the EU MiCA regulation and most regions worldwide have not yet issued a clear definition of DeFi, leaving room for regulatory arbitrage in the industry. He stated that DeFi is currently in a regulatory honeymoon period, and regulators will most likely regulate decentralized exchanges (DEXs) first. Meanwhile, centralized exchanges (CEXs) are now subject to bank-level compliance reviews, which is significantly different from the threshold-free and anonymous trading environment of DEXs. The full implementation of DeFi regulation will still take time. — link
author: admin#1534592031
